betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Nuclear Waste§f:
Nuclear Waste is the radioactive byproduct of running a Solid Fission Reactor. Spent fuel is produced when LEU/HEU/MOX fuel is fully depleted in the reactor core.

§6Depleted Fuel Forms (FuelReprocessorRecipes.java:64-67)§f:
When fuel is consumed, it becomes one of the following:
- §bDepleted LEU-235 Oxide Pellet§f (nuclearcraft:depleted_fuel_uranium, meta 9) — the most common output of an LEU-235 oxide fuel cycle
- Depleted HEU-235 Oxide Pellet (meta 11) — high-enriched equivalent
- Depleted LEU-235 Carbide Pellet (meta 8)
- Depleted LEU-235 Nitride Pellet (meta 10)
- Depleted LEU-235 ZA Pellet (meta 12) — zirconium-alloy cladding
- Plus parallel forms for U-233, Np-236, Pu-239/241, Am-242, Cm-243/245/247, Bk-248, Cf-249/251 fuels

§6What Comes Out of Depleted Fuel (FuelReprocessorRecipes.java:62-68)§f:
The Fuel Reprocessor breaks depleted fuel back into useful isotopes:
- 4 main actinide products (fertile + 1-3 fissile isotopes)
- 2 radioactive waste dusts (Strontium-90, Cesium-137, Molybdenum, Promethium-147, Ruthenium-106, or Europium-155, depending on fuel family)
- Dust Graphite (for TRISO) or Dust Zirconium (for ZA) as bonus outputs
- Dust Silicon Carbide (for TRISO) as a structural recovery

§6Direct Descendants of Depleted LEU-235 Oxide (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The graph shows 6 items that use depleted fuel:
- Uranium-238 Oxide (recovered fertile material)
- Plutonium-239 Oxide (recovered fissile material)
- Plutonium-242 Oxide (recovered fertile material)
- Tiny Pile of Molybdenum Powder (waste dust, contenttweaker)
- Tiny Pile of Caesium-137 Powder (waste dust, hbm)
- Americium-243 Oxide (recovered fertile material)
- Dirty Bomb (trinity) — anti-structure weapon using radioactive material

§6Waste Management§f:
Depleted fuel is intensely radioactive. Store it in:
- A shielded container (NC:rad_shielding)
- A Rad-Shielded Chest (NC container variant)
- A dedicated underground storage room lined with DU Plating

§6Spent Fuel Pools§f:
NC's best practice is a §bSpent Fuel Pool§f: a water-filled containment cell where depleted fuel can be safely stored long-term. Water provides passive cooling and partial radiation shielding.

§6Recycling Path§f:
- Depleted fuel -> Fuel Reprocessor -> recoverable actinides + waste dusts
- Recovered actinides -> Isotope Separator -> enriched/fertile streams
- Waste dusts -> Decay Hastener -> useful isotopes (Cesium-137 for RTGs, Strontium-90 for betavoltaics)
- Waste dusts -> Melter -> fluid form for further chemical processing

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30043 (Solid Fission Reactor) — without a reactor, no fuel is consumed and no waste is produced.

§eTip§f: Set up a Fuel Reprocessor line as soon as you start burning fuel. Recycling 9 depleted pellets back into 1 enriched + 8 fertile + 2 wastes dramatically reduces the amount of fresh mining you need.

§eTip§f: Don't dump depleted fuel in lava or fire — it will not destroy the radiation and may cause a radioactive contamination event. Use a proper disposal chain.

§cWarning§f: Depleted fuel emits lethal radiation at close range. Always handle it with a full hazmat suit + respirator, and never carry more than a single stack in your inventory.
